channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: European defense heavyweights say meeting Trump's military spending target won't be easy
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — The defense ministers of Europe's five top military spenders said Monday they want to continue increasing their investments in defense but described meeting President-elect Donald Trump's challenge for them to raise spending to 5% of their overall economic output as complicated
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA and MONIKA SCISLOWSKA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Naked, ash-smeared Hindu ascetics take holy dips at the Maha Kumbh festival
item.description: PRAYAGRAJ, India (AP) — Naked Hindu ascetics and pilgrims took dips in the freezing waters at the confluence of sacred rivers in northern India on Tuesday, in the first major bathing day of the Maha Kumbh festival, which is the largest religious congregation on Earth.
item.dc:creator: SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: China reportedly building 'D-Day'-style barges as fears of Taiwan invasion rise
item.description: China is reportedly building a series of barges for amphibious landings amid fears of an invasion of Taiwan, its island neighbor, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A missile fired by Houthi rebels targets central Israel as airstrikes hit displaced area in Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — A missile fired by Yemen’s Houthi rebels targeted central Israel early Tuesday, causing sirens to blare and people to flee into bomb shelters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some Israeli soldiers refuse to keep fighting in Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Yotam Vilk says the image of Israeli soldiers killing an unarmed Palestinian teenager in the Gaza Strip is seared in his mind. An officer in the armored corps, Vilk said the instructions were to shoot any unauthorized person who entered an Israeli-controlled buffer zone in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: North Korea launches missiles toward eastern waters in 2nd launch of year, South says
item.description: South Korea’s military says North Korea has test-fired multiple missiles toward its eastern waters in its second launch event of 2025.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Canada ready to buy more American products to appease Trump's tariff threat, ambassador says
item.description: PALM DESERT, Calif. (AP) — Canada's ambassador to Washington said Monday that Canada is prepared to buy more from the United States, including its next fleet of submarines, to appease President-elect Donald Trump's concerns about the U.S.-Canada trade deficit.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No. 2 Senate Democratic leader will back Rubio for secretary of State
item.description: Senate Democratic Whip Dick Durbin (Ill.) on Monday announced that he will vote for Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.) to become the next secretary of State, a sign that Rubio is on a path to be confirmed on President-elect Trump’s first day of office with overwhelming support.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: At least 100 illegal miners have died while trapped in a South African mine for months, group says
item.description: JOHANNESBURG (AP) — At least 100 men who were mining illegally in an abandoned gold mine in South Africa have died of suspected starvation and dehydration after being trapped deep underground for months while police tried to force them out, a group representing the miners said Monday.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden says he’s leaving Trump ‘strong hand to play,' defends his record on Afghanistan
item.description: Biden spoke for the last time on U.S. foreign policy and national security, and an issue seen as one of his greatest blunders, the withdrawal from Afghanistan, did not escape mentioning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italian photographer Oliviero Toscani, famed for provocative Benetton campaigns, dies at 82
item.description: MILAN (AP) — Oliviero Toscani, the photographer behind Benetton’s provocative ad campaigns of the 1980s and 1990s who later broke with the Italian knitwear brand amid controversy, died Monday at age 82. Toscani disclosed last year he had a rare disease.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch live: Biden delivers foreign address at State Department
item.description: President Biden began his farewell tour around Washington this week with an address on Monday at the State Department to highlight his work on foreign policy during his presidency. Biden was expected to defend his foreign policy legacy, including reconnecting the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Head of the International Court of Justice is named Lebanon's new prime minister
item.description: Lebanon’s new president has asked prominent diplomat and jurist Nawaf Salam to form the country’s new government after Salam was named prime minister by a large number of legislators.
item.dc:creator: By Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Baby gorilla finds home at Istanbul zoo after rescue
item.description: Five-month-old Zeytin is recovering at an Istanbul zoo after being rescued from a cargo hold on a Turkish Airlines flight.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Russian forces bypass a key stronghold in a bid to cut off its supplies, a Ukrainian officer says
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian forces are bypassing a key stronghold in eastern Ukraine that they have fought for months to capture and are focusing instead on cutting supply lines to it, a Ukrainian official said Monday.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and YEHOR KONOVALOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Putin call expected soon, Waltz says
item.description: President-elect Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin are expected to have a phone call in the coming days or weeks, Trump’s incoming national security adviser, Rep. Mike Waltz (R-Fla.), said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Biden calls for immediate cease-fire in call with Israel's Netanyahu
item.description: President Biden and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu held a phone call on Sunday to discuss the ongoing ceasefire and hostage release negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about the latest effort to bring an end to Turkey's 40-year Kurdish conflict
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Talks between politicians from Turkey’s pro-Kurdish party and jailed Kurdish leaders have been gathering steam as they try to end 40 years of fighting between the state and the Kurdistan Workers’ Party, or PKK.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 1 poll finds majority of Greenland respondents support joining US
item.description: A new survey found that a majority of Greenland respondents support joining the United States. According to a poll by Patriot Polling released Sunday, 57.3 percent of respondents approve of Greenland becoming part of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Canada deploying 60 firefighters to help with California blazes
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced Sunday he was deploying 60 firefighters to California to help with the wildfires. In a post on the social platform X, Trudeau said Canada was helping aid the Los Angeles area as several wildfires continue devastating the area.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Canada's Trudeau urges US consumers to consider the harm of Trump's tariff threats
item.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia (AP) — Canada's outgoing Prime Minister Justin Trudeau on Sunday suggested that President-elect Donald Trump's remarks about Canada becoming America's “51st state” has distracted attention from the harm that steep tariffs would inflict on U.S. consumers.
item.dc:creator: JIM MORRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No pants? No problem. London subway riders bare their legs to brighten a gray winter day
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Mind the pants. Hundreds of Londoners headed down to the Underground on Sunday afternoon, stripped down to their underwear and travelled around a bit, trying to look as though nothing unusual was going on. As if.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Black boxes from South Korea plane crash failed to record final 4 minutes, officials say
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The black boxes of a Boeing jetliner that crashed in South Korea last month stopped recording about four minutes before the accident, South Korean officials said Saturday, possibly complicating investigations into the cause of the disaster that killed 179 people.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump issues warning to Maduro as Venezuelan leader enters third term, US expands sanctions
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump issued a warning to Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro, who entered his third term Friday, that he better keep democracy activists 'safe and alive' after detaining Maria Machado.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's victory could give boost to Brazil's Bolsonaro's re-election hopes amid court ban on him running
item.description: Brazil's supreme court has prevented Jair Bolsonaro from running again in the 2026 presidential election, yet the former president and his team are hoping the incoming Trump administration might help.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Details of Venezuelan opposition leader's possible arrest remain unclear amid Maduro inauguration resistance
item.description: Venezuelan opposition leader Maria Corina Machado's aides claim she was detained Thursday, despite denials by the country's socialist government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thousands of Venezuelan opposition supporters take to the streets ahead of Maduro's third inauguration
item.description: Venezuelan opposition supporters protested around the country in a last-minute effort to put pressure on President Maduro the day before he is to be sworn in for his third term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel sends report to UN on 'brutal' treatment used by terrorists against hostages in Gaza
item.description: In a new report sent to the U.N. by Israel's health ministry, freed hostages reveal systematic abuse by Hamas, as 99 remain in captivity enduring brutal physical and psychological torment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
